rulings.Case Title: UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. ADRIANA PATRICIA A. BARAJAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Set Aside And Vacate Entry Of Dismissal
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 10, 2009, line 1, PLAINTIFF UNIVERSITY OF SAN FRANCISCO MOTION To Set Aside And Vacate Entry Of Dismissal. Off calendar. Motion to be calendared in Department 212 before Commissioner Borick because she is the judicial officer who ordered the dismissal. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JIMMY DOMINO VS. SAN FRANCISCO CHRONICLE
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 10, 2009, line 2, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO CHRONICLE, A DIVISION OF HEARST MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion Granted. The undisputed evidence shows that (1) an essential function of plaintiff's job was to lift the bundles; (2) plaintiff could not perform his job, including the lifting, through March 2004; (3) defendant attempted to contact plaintiff in March 2004 regarding a reasonable accommodation; (4) plaintiff did not properly respond to defendant's inquiries; (5) plaintiff failed to ask for or show how defendant could have accommodated plaintiff; and (6) defendant properly terminated plaintiff because he could not perform the essential functions of his job. =(302/CWW/PB)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT STUDENT VS. HAIGHT ASHBURY FREE CLINICS, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 10, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT HAIGHT ASHBURY FREE CLINICS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Motion granted in part, and denied in part. Motion Granted as to 2nd Cause of Action for retaliation and 3rd cause of action for breach of contract. No opposition filed. Motion Denied as to 1st, 4th, 5th and 6th causes of action. Triable issues of fact exist concerning whether plaintiff was performing his job satisfactorily, and whether plaintiff was terminated for his conduct or for his complaints about the clinic's allegedly illegal activities. Factual dispute exists regarding whether plaintiff worked under the direction of Sullivan or with discretion and independent judgment, and whether plaintiff was engaged in intellectual and creative work. =(302/CWW/ LT)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL R JOHNSON VS. KORBIN FAMILY LAW

rulings.Case Title: US PARKING INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO A MUNICIPAL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 10, 2009, line 7, PLAINTIFF US PARKING INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, CIHAT ESREFOGLU'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Motion for preliminary Injuction is Denied. Plaintiffs fail to demonstrate that they are likely to prevail on the merits. Plaintiffs received a hearing before the Tax Collector. Plaintiffs' contentions that Article 6.18-2 is invalid and that the City Tax Code is unconstitutional are not supported by case law. San Francisco's summary judgment collection procedure is not preempted. Post payment judicial review is a clear and certain remedy satisfying due process. =(302/CWW/JW)

rulings.Case Title: BARBARA KELLY et al VS. CESAR ESPINOZA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Set Aside Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, February 10, 2009, line 13, DEFENDANT MARIA (SUED AS DOE 1) URIBE'S Motion to Set Aside Judgment. Relief is granted in part, and denied in part. Motion to set aside judgment is Denied. No basis to set aside judgment against Cesar Espinoza. The writ of execution for possession of the premises is recalled and quashed. Maria Uribe must file her prejudgment claim of right to possession on or before February 13, 2009. =(302/CWW/PB)
